title: asperula glomerata subsp. filiformis
intro: Asperula glomerata subsp. filiformis, commonly known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and narrow, elongated leaves. Traditionally, Woodruff has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The plant has been found to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Woodruff is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and can be used to make traditional herbal preparations, such as Woodruff schnapps, a sweet, fragrant liqueur traditionally enjoyed during German festivals.
